description: Valid hook configuration for Claude Code

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds. Defaults: command=600, prompt=30, agent=60

statusMessage.description: Custom spinner message shown while the hook runs

once.description: If true, runs only once per session then removed (skills only)

async.description: Run hook in background without blocking Claude. Only for command type.

url.description: URL to send POST request to

headers.description: HTTP headers with env var interpolation ($VAR or ${VAR})

allowedEnvVars.description: Environment variable names allowed in headers

prompt.description: Text prompt for single-turn LLM evaluation. Use $ARGUMENTS for hook input JSON

model.description: Model to use for evaluation (default: fast model)

prompt.description: Text prompt for multi-turn agent verification. Use $ARGUMENTS for hook input JSON

model.description: Model to use for the agent (default: fast model)

UserPromptSubmit.note: Matcher is ignored for this event

Stop.note: Matcher is ignored for this event

TeammateIdle.description: Agent team teammate about to go idle
TeammateIdle.note: No matcher support. Exit 2 only (no hookSpecificOutput support)

TaskCompleted.note: No matcher support. Exit 2 only (no hookSpecificOutput support)

ConfigChange.note: Matcher is the config type that changed

InstructionsLoaded.description: When CLAUDE.md or .claude/rules/*.md file is loaded
InstructionsLoaded.note: No matcher support. Exit code is ignored.

WorktreeCreate.description: When a worktree is being created
WorktreeCreate.note: No matcher support. Any non-zero exit fails worktree creation. Hook prints absolute path to worktree on stdout.

WorktreeRemove.description: When a worktree is being removed
WorktreeRemove.note: No matcher support. Receives worktree_path in input. Logged in debug mode only.

Elicitation.description: When an MCP server requests user input during a tool call
Elicitation.note: Matcher is the MCP server name (regex). Exit 2 denies elicitation.

ElicitationResult.description: After a user responds to an MCP elicitation
ElicitationResult.note: Matcher is the MCP server name (regex). Exit 2 blocks response.

updatedInput.description: PreToolUse hooks can modify tool inputs before execution
updatedInput.usage: Return JSON with hookSpecificOutput.updatedInput to rewrite tool arguments

permissionDecision.description: PermissionRequest hooks can control permission decisions
permissionDecision.usage: Return hookSpecificOutput.decision with behavior (allow/deny), updatedInput, updatedPermissions, message

topLevelDecision.description: Several events support top-level decision/reason for blocking

elicitationAction.description: Elicitation hooks can control the action

universalFields.description: All events support these stdout JSON fields

fields.continue: If false, Claude stops entirely (takes precedence over event-specific fields)
fields.stopReason: Message shown to user when continue is false
fields.suppressOutput: If true, hides stdout from verbose mode

promptAgentResponse.description: Prompt and agent hook types return structured responses

exitCodes.2: Block the action (stderr becomes Claude's feedback)
exitCodes.other: Allow but log error (visible in verbose mode)

blocking.description: These events can be blocked with exit code 2

nonBlocking.description: Exit code 2 shows stderr but does not block
nonBlocking.note: WorktreeCreate: any non-zero exit fails creation. WorktreeRemove: logged in debug only.

hookInput.description: Hook commands receive a JSON object via stdin (NOT via environment variables). HTTP hooks receive the same JSON as POST body.

structure.permission_mode: Current permission mode (default, plan, acceptEdits, dontAsk, bypassPermissions)
structure.hook_event_name: The event that triggered the hook (e.g. PostToolUse)
structure.tool_name: Name of the tool (for PreToolUse/PostToolUse events)
structure.tool_input: Object with the tool's input parameters (e.g. file_path, command)
structure.tool_response: Object with the tool's response (PostToolUse only)
structure.tool_use_id: Unique identifier for the tool invocation
structure.agent_id: Subagent ID (only when hook fires inside subagent call)
structure.agent_type: Agent name (only with --agent or inside subagent)

hookInput.note: CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT and TOOL_INPUT env vars are NOT set. Always read from stdin.

environmentVariables.description: These env vars are available but do NOT include tool input
environmentVariables.CLAUDE_ENV_FILE: File path for persisting environment variables (SessionStart hooks)
environmentVariables.CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT: Root directory of the plugin (for referencing bundled scripts)
environmentVariables.CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE: Set to 'true' when running in remote/web environments

invalidEvents.description: These event names DO NOT EXIST - never use them
